content:
# Sharawra Formation

**Summary**: A Silurian sedimentary formation in Saudi Arabia consisting of sandstone and siltstone.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] studied the Sharawra Formation to evaluate its reservoir potential.

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] analyzed the Sharawra Member as a significant siliciclastic reservoir unit.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] prodeltaic / shallow marine

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] analyzed the Sharawra Member as a significant siliciclastic reservoir unit.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] compaction, cementation (iron oxide, calcite, silica), and clay alteration

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The average porosity for the sandstone facies is 15 percent, while siltstones range around 7 percent. ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 19]])

- The Sharawra Member shows a coarsening upward trend, transitioning from silty to sandier sections. ([[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024, p. 17]])
